title: Horsfordia newberryi
intro: Horsfordia newberryi is a medicinal plant species, commonly known as the 'Desert Honeysuckle', native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Horsfordia newberryi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil to treat various ailments, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with medications and allergies.
taxonomy: Horsfordia newberryi is a species within the family Malvaceae. This flowering plant is native to the southwestern United States. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree. The classification of Horsfordia newberryi places it in the order Malvales and the clade Rosids. Further, it belongs to the clade Angiosperms and the kingdom Plantae.
